metadata.title:
CHRISTMAS PARTIES FOR


TWO SPOKANE CLUBS

pages.raw_text:
One fthe very charming Christ
mas functions of the holiday sea-
son was that given yesterday by
Mrs. G. Fl. Wright to the members
Of the Athenaeum club at the
Wright home on Pacific avenue.
Decorations were prettily apropos
Of Christmas time. Beautiful place
cards sent by the absent president
of the club. Mrs. w. r. Week, from
Germany were distributed. and the
guests received the annual gifts by
reciting a Christmas rhyme, A box
Of good things in the way of games,
toys and necessary articles was
made up and sent to the Parental
school.


The Amethyst club was delight
fully entertained yesterday after
noon at the home Of Mrs. J. Grier
Long. 1924 Eighth avenue. In ad-
dition to the luncheon and distri-
button of gifts from a large Christ
mas tree, a musical program of en-
joyable merit was rendered.


GIVE DELIGHTFUL MUSICALE


AT GIRLS SCHOOL.


About 200 guests were present at
the musicale given by Brunot Hall
students in the music room of the
school last evening. An excellent
and extensive program rendered by
the pupils was pleasureably pre-
scented. consisting of solos and
choruses. with a reading or two.
The closing chorus of "Hark! the
Christmas Bells Are Ringing" was
well sung and lent dainty Holt
day touch.


DOMESTIC SCIENCE DEPART
MENT TO MEET TODAY.


Members of the Domestic Science
department of the Woman's club
will gather in the club home this
afternoon to listen to q lecture by
the instructor, Miss Rogers, upon
breakfast dishes. Omelets, muf-
fins and coffee will be studied and
experimental upon and business
matters of Importance are to be
considered.


WILL SPEAK UPON PHILIP.


PINES.


Mrs. w. j. Rhynesberger will
give stereopticon lecture at the
First Presbyterian church this eve.
ming upon the Philippine islands.
